records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4239 - The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"Vessels of gold and silver are the possessions of those who do not have certainty (in faith)."
records.arabicText: 4239 - وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "آنِيَةُ اَلذَّهَبِ وَ اَلْفِضَّةِ مَتَاعُ "اَلَّذِينَ لاٰ يُوقِنُونَ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4239 - The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Vessels of gold and silver are the possessions of those who do not have certainty (in faith)."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4240 - Yunus bin Yaqub narrated from his brother Yusuf:
Abu Abdullah (as), once asked for water, and a vessel made of brass containing water was brought to him.
One of those sitting with him said: "'Abbad al-Basri dislikes drinking from brass vessels."
Imam (as) said: "Then ask him, is it gold or silver?"
records.arabicText: 4240 - وَ رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ عَنْ يُوسُفَ أَخِيهِ : أَنَّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اِسْتَسْقَى مَاءً فَأُتِيَ بِقَدَحٍ مِنْ صُفْرٍ فِيهِ مَاءٌ فَقَالَ لَهُ بَعْضُ جُلَسَائِهِ إِنَّ عَبَّادَ اَلْبَصْرِيَّ يَكْرَهُ اَلشُّرْبَ فِي اَلصُّفْرِ قَالَ "فَسَلْهُ أَ ذَهَبٌ هُوَ أَمْ فِضَّةٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4240 - Yunus bin Yaqub narrated from his brother Yusuf:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Abu Abdullah (as), once asked for water, and a vessel made of brass containing water was brought to him.
One of those sitting with him said: "'Abbad al-Basri dislikes drinking from brass vessels."
Imam (as) said: "Then ask him, is it gold or silver?"

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4241 - It is narrated from Jarrah Al-Mada'ini:
Abu Abdullah (as), disliked that a man should eat with his left hand, drink with it, or take anything with it.
records.arabicText: 4241 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ جَرَّاحٍ اَلْمَدَائِنِيِّ قَالَ : كَرِهَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ اَلرَّجُلُ بِشِمَالِهِ أَوْ يَشْرَبَ بِهَا أَوْ يَتَنَاوَلَ بِهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4241 - It is narrated from Jarrah Al-Mada'ini:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Abu Abdullah (as), disliked that a man should eat with his left hand, drink with it, or take anything with it.

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4242 - It is narrated by Abdullah bin Maymoon, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
The companions of the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, were drinking water in large gulps at Tabuk.
So the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, said:
"Drink with your hands, for they are among the best of your vessels."
records.arabicText: 4242 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ مَيْمُونٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "كَانَ أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ بِتَبُوكَ يَعُبُّونَ اَلْمَاءَ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "اِشْرَبُوا فِي أَيْدِيكُمْ فَإِنَّهَا مِنْ خَيْرِ آنِيَتِكُمْ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4242 - It is narrated by Abdullah bin Maymoon, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
The companions of the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, were drinking water in large gulps at Tabuk.
So the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, said:
"Drink with your hands, for they are among the best of your vessels."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4243 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
"Drinking water while standing during the day increases perspiration and strengthens the body."
records.arabicText: 4243 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "شُرْبُ اَلْمَاءِ مِنْ قِيَامٍ بِالنَّهَارِ أَدَرُّ لِلْعِرْقِ وَ أَقْوَى لِلْبَدَنِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4243 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Drinking water while standing during the day increases perspiration and strengthens the body."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4244 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
"Drinking water while standing at night causes yellow water (jaundice)."
records.arabicText: 4244 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "شُرْبُ اَلْمَاءِ بِاللَّيْلِ مِنْ قِيَامٍ يُورِثُ اَلْمَاءَ اَلْأَصْفَرَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4244 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Drinking water while standing at night causes yellow water (jaundice)."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4245 - One of his companions asked him about drinking water in a single breath.
Imam (as) said: "If the person handing you the water is your servant, then drink it in three breaths; but if he is free, then drink it in a single breath."
records.arabicText: 4245 - و سَأَلَهُ بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِهِ : عَنِ اَلشُّرْبِ بِنَفَسٍ وَاحِدٍ فَقَالَ "إِذَا كَانَ اَلَّذِي يُنَاوِلُكَ اَلْمَاءَ مَمْلُوكاً لَكَ فَاشْرَبْ فِي ثَلاَثَةِ أَنْفَاسٍ وَ إِنْ كَانَ حُرّاً فَاشْرَبْهُ بِنَفَسٍ وَاحِدٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4245 - One of his companions asked him about drinking water in a single breath.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the person handing you the water is your servant, then drink it in three breaths; but if he is free, then drink it in a single breath."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4246 - In a narration by Hammad from Al-Halabi, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said: "Drinking in three breaths is better than drinking in one breath."
Imam (as) disliked that a person should resemble 'Al-Heem.'
I asked: "What is Al-Heem?"
Imam (as) replied: "It refers to camels."
In another narration, it states: "Al-Heem refers to thirsty camels."
And it is also narrated: "Al-Heem refers to those upon whom the name of Allah (swt) has not been mentioned."
records.arabicText:
4246 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَمَّادٍ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "ثَلاَثَةُ أَنْفَاسٍ فِي اَلشُّرْبِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ شُرْبٍ بِنَفَسٍ وَاحِدٍ" وَ كَانَ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُشَبَّهَ بِالْهِيمِ قُلْتُ وَ مَا اَلْهِيمُ قَالَ "اَلزِّمْلُ". وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ: اَلْإِبِلُ.
وَ رُوِيَ: "أَنَّ اَلْهِيمَ اَلنِّيبُ". وَ رُوِيَ: "أَنَّ اَلْهِيمَ مَا "لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اِسْمُ اَللّٰهِ عَلَيْهِ" "
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4246 - In a narration by Hammad from Al-Halabi, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Drinking in three breaths is better than drinking in one breath."
Imam (as) disliked that a person should resemble 'Al-Heem.'
I asked: "What is Al-Heem?"
Imam (as) replied: "It refers to camels."
In another narration, it states: "Al-Heem refers to thirsty camels."
And it is also narrated: "Al-Heem refers to those upon whom the name of Allah (swt) has not been mentioned."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4247 - Abdullah ibn Al-Mughirah narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), that he said:
"Do not eat while you are walking unless you are compelled to do so."
records.arabicText: 4247 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ تَأْكُلْ وَ أَنْتَ تَمْشِي إِلاَّ أَنْ تُضْطَرَّ إِلَى ذَلِكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4247 - Abdullah ibn Al-Mughirah narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not eat while you are walking unless you are compelled to do so."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4248 - It is narrated from Umar ibn Abi Shu'bah who said:
"I saw Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) eating while reclining.
Then Imam (as) mentioned the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, and said: 'He never ate while reclining until he passed away.' "
records.arabicText: 4248 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ أَبِي شُعْبَةَ قَالَ : رَأَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَأْكُلُ مُتَّكِئاً ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَقَالَ "مَا أَكَلَ مُتَّكِئاً حَتَّى مَاتَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4248 - It is narrated from Umar ibn Abi Shu'bah who said:
"I saw Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) eating while reclining.
Then Imam (as) mentioned the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'He never ate while reclining until he passed away.' "

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4249 - It is narrated from Hammad ibn Uthman, from Umar ibn Abi Shu'bah, from Abi Shu'bah:
"He saw Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), eating while sitting cross-legged."
records.arabicText: 4249 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ أَبِي شُعْبَةَ عَنْ أَبِي شُعْبَةَ : أَنَّهُ رَأَى أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَأْكُلُ مُتَرَبِّعاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4249 - It is narrated from Hammad ibn Uthman, from Umar ibn Abi Shu'bah, from Abi Shu'bah:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He saw Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), eating while sitting cross-legged."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4250 - It is narrated by Isma'il ibn Abi Ziyad, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"When the table (for food) is set, four angels surround it.
If the servant says 'In the name of Allah (swt),' the angels say to Satan, 'Be gone, O wicked one, for you have no authority over them.'
But if they finish eating and say 'Praise be to Allah (swt),' the angels say, 'They are a people whom Allah (swt) has blessed, and they have expressed gratitude to their Lord (azj).'
However, if they do not say 'In the name of Allah (swt),' the angels say to Satan, 'Come closer, O wicked one, and eat with them.'
And if they do not praise Allah (swt) when they finish, the angels say, 'They are a people whom Allah (swt) has blessed, but they forgot their Lord (azj).'"
records.arabicText: 4250 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ : "إِذَا وُضِعَتِ اَلْمَائِدَةُ حَفَّهَا أَرْبَعَةُ أَمْلاَكٍ فَإِذَا قَالَ اَلْعَبْدُ بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ قَالَتِ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ لِلشَّيْطَانِ اِخْزَ يَا فَاسِقُ فَلاَ سُلْطَانَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ فَإِذَا فَرَغُوا فَقَالُوا اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ قَالَتِ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ هُمْ قَوْمٌ أَنْعَمَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ فَأَدَّوْا شُكْرَ رَبِّهِمْ فَإِذَا لَمْ يَقُولُوا بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ قَالَتِ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ لِلشَّيْطَانِ اُدْنُ يَا فَاسِقُ فَكُلْ مَعَهُمْ فَإِذَا رُفِعَتْ فَلَمْ يَحْمَدُوا اَللَّهَ قَالَتِ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ هُمْ قَوْمٌ أَنْعَمَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ فَنَسُوا رَبَّهُمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4250 - It is narrated by Isma'il ibn Abi Ziyad, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When the table (for food) is set, four angels surround it.
If the servant says 'In the name of Allah (swt),' the angels say to Satan, 'Be gone, O wicked one, for you have no authority over them.'
But if they finish eating and say 'Praise be to Allah (swt),' the angels say, 'They are a people whom Allah (swt) has blessed, and they have expressed gratitude to their Lord (azj).'
However, if they do not say 'In the name of Allah (swt),' the angels say to Satan, 'Come closer, O wicked one, and eat with them.'
And if they do not praise Allah (swt) when they finish, the angels say, 'They are a people whom Allah (swt) has blessed, but they forgot their Lord (azj).'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4251 - The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
"The owner of the provision will drink first among the people and performs ablution last among them."
records.arabicText: 4251 - وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "صَاحِبُ اَلرَّحْلِ يَشْرَبُ أَوَّلَ اَلْقَوْمِ وَ يَتَوَضَّأُ آخِرَهُمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4251 - The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The owner of the provision will drink first among the people and performs ablution last among them."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4252 - Sama'ah bin Mehran narrated:
I was eating with Abu Abdullah (as), and Imam (as) said:
"O Sama'ah, eat with gratitude and praise, not with eating and silence."
records.arabicText: 4252 - وَ رَوَى سَمَاعَةُ بْنُ مِهْرَانَ قَالَ : كُنْتُ آكُلُ مَعَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ "يَا سَمَاعَةُ أَكْلاً وَ حَمْداً لاَ أَكْلاً وَ صَمْتاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4252 - Sama'ah bin Mehran narrated:
I was eating with Abu Abdullah (as), and Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "O Sama'ah, eat with gratitude and praise, not with eating and silence."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4253 - Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, said:
"I guarantee that whoever mentions the name of Allah (swt) upon his food will not suffer any harm from it."
Then Ibn al-Kawwa' said:
"O Commander of the Faithful, I ate food last night, mentioned the name of Allah (swt) upon it, and yet it caused me discomfort."
Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, replied:
"You ate different types of food, but you mentioned the name of Allah (swt) on some and did not mention it on others, O foolish one!"
It is also narrated: "Whoever forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt) on each type of food should say: 'In the name of Allah (swt), on its beginning and its end.'"
records.arabicText:
4253 - وَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "ضَمِنْتُ لِمَنْ سَمَّى عَلَى طَعَامِهِ أَنْ لاَ يَشْتَكِيَ مِنْهُ" فَقَالَ اِبْنُ اَلْكَوَّاءِ يَا أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَقَدْ أَكَلْتُ اَلْبَارِحَةَ طَعَاماً فَسَمَّيْتُ عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ آذَانِي فَقَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "أَكَلْتَ أَلْوَاناً فَسَمَّيْتَ عَلَى بَعْضِهَا وَ لَمْ تُسَمِّ عَلَى بَعْضٍ يَا لُكَعُ".
وَ رُوِيَ: "أَنَّ مَنْ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُسَمِّيَ عَلَى كُلِّ لَوْنٍ فَلْيَقُلْ بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ عَلَى أَوَّلِهِ وَ آخِرِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4253 - Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I guarantee that whoever mentions the name of Allah (swt) upon his food will not suffer any harm from it."
Then Ibn al-Kawwa' said:
"O Commander of the Faithful, I ate food last night, mentioned the name of Allah (swt) upon it, and yet it caused me discomfort."
Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, replied:
"You ate different types of food, but you mentioned the name of Allah (swt) on some and did not mention it on others, O foolish one!"
It is also narrated: "Whoever forgets to mention the name of Allah (swt) on each type of food should say: 'In the name of Allah (swt), on its beginning and its end.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4254 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
"I have never suffered from indigestion, and that is because I never began eating any food without saying: 'In the name of Allah (swt),' and I never finished eating without saying: 'All praise is due to Allah (swt).'"
records.arabicText: 4254 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَا اِتَّخَمْتُ قَطُّ وَ ذَلِكَ أَنِّي لَمْ أَبْدَأْ بِطَعَامٍ إِلاَّ قُلْتُ: بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ وَ لَمْ أَفْرُغْ مِنْ طَعَامٍ إِلاَّ قُلْتُ اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4254 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I have never suffered from indigestion, and that is because I never began eating any food without saying: 'In the name of Allah (swt),' and I never finished eating without saying: 'All praise is due to Allah (swt).'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4255 - Imam (as) said: "Indeed, when the stomach is full, it becomes rebellious."
records.arabicText: 4255 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ اَلْبَطْنَ إِذَا شَبِعَ طَغَى".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4255 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, when the stomach is full, it becomes rebellious."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4256 - Amr ibn Qays al-Masir narrated:
I entered upon Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), in Medina, and there was a dining table before him, and Imam (as) was eating.
I said to Imam (as): "What is the proper etiquette for this table?"
Imam (as) replied: "When you place it, mention the name of Allah (swt), and when you remove it, praise Allah (swt). Clean up what is around the table, for this is its etiquette."
He said: Then I turned, and there was a jug placed there. So I said to Imam (as), "What is the proper etiquette for the jug?"
Imam (as) replied: "Drink from the side of its lip, and mention the name of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic. When you lift it from your mouth, praise Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic. Avoid drinking from the spot near the handle, for that is the seat of Satan. This is its etiquette."
records.arabicText: 4256 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ قَيْسٍ اَلْمَاصِرِ قَالَ : دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِالْمَدِينَةِ وَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ خِوَانٌ وَ هُوَ يَأْكُلُ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ مَا حَدُّ هَذَا اَلْخِوَانِ فَقَالَ "إِذَا وَضَعْتَهُ فَسَمِّ اَللَّهَ وَ إِذَا رَفَعْتَهُ فَاحْمَدِ اَللَّهَ وَ قُمَّ مَا حَوْلَ اَلْخِوَانِ فَإِنَّ هَذَا حَدُّهُ" قَالَ فَالْتَفَتُّ فَإِذَا كُوزٌ مَوْضُوعٌ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ مَا حَدُّ اَلْكُوزِ فَقَالَ "اِشْرَبْ مِمَّا يَلِي شَفَتَيْهِ وَ سَمِّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَإِذَا رَفَعْتَهُ عَنْ فِيكَ فَاحْمَدِ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ إِيَّاكَ وَ مَوْضِعَ اَلْعُرْوَةِ أَنْ تَشْرَبَ مِنْهَا فَإِنَّهَا مَقْعَدُ اَلشَّيْطَانِ فَهَذَا حَدُّهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4256 - Amr ibn Qays al-Masir narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I entered upon Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), in Medina, and there was a dining table before him, and Imam (as) was eating.
I said to Imam (as): "What is the proper etiquette for this table?"
Imam (as) replied: "When you place it, mention the name of Allah (swt), and when you remove it, praise Allah (swt). Clean up what is around the table, for this is its etiquette."
He said: Then I turned, and there was a jug placed there. So I said to Imam (as), "What is the proper etiquette for the jug?"
Imam (as) replied: "Drink from the side of its lip, and mention the name of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic. When you lift it from your mouth, praise Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic. Avoid drinking from the spot near the handle, for that is the seat of Satan. This is its etiquette."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4257 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Walid al-Kirmani:
I ate in the presence of Abu Ja'far al-Thani Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Jawad (as).
When I finished and the dining mat was lifted, a servant went to collect the crumbs of food that had fallen.
Imam (as) said to him: "Whatever is in the open area, leave it even if it is the leg of a sheep, and whatever is inside the house, collect it and pick it up."
records.arabicText: 4257 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْوَلِيدِ اَلْكِرْمَانِيِّ قَالَ : أَكَلْتُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ اَلثَّانِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ حَتَّى إِذَا فَرَغْتُ وَ رُفِعَ اَلْخِوَانُ ذَهَبَ اَلْغُلاَمُ يَرْفَعُ مَا وَقَعَ مِنْ فُتَاتِ اَلطَّعَامِ فَقَالَ لَهُ "مَا كَانَ فِي اَلصَّحْرَاءِ فَدَعْهُ وَ لَوْ فَخِذَ شَاةٍ وَ مَا كَانَ فِي اَلْبَيْتِ فَتَتَبَّعْهُ وَ اُلْقُطْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4257 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Walid al-Kirmani:
I ate in the presence of Abu Ja'far al-Thani Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Jawad (as).
records.thaqalaynMatn:
When I finished and the dining mat was lifted, a servant went to collect the crumbs of food that had fallen.
Imam (as) said to him: "Whatever is in the open area, leave it even if it is the leg of a sheep, and whatever is inside the house, collect it and pick it up."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4258 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), said:
"Indeed, the Banu Umayyah begin their meals with vinegar and end them with salt. Whereas we begin our meals with salt and end them with vinegar."
records.arabicText: 4258 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ بَنِي أُمَيَّةَ يَبْدَءُونَ بِالْخَلِّ فِي أَوَّلِ اَلطَّعَامِ وَ يَخْتِمُونَ بِالْمِلْحِ وَ إِنَّا نَبْدَأُ بِالْمِلْحِ فِي أَوَّلِ اَلطَّعَامِ وَ نَخْتِمُ بِالْخَلِّ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4259 - Commander of the Faithful (as) said: "Begin with salt at the start of the meal, for if people knew what is in salt, they would prefer it over the tried remedy."
records.arabicText: 4259 - وَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اِبْدَءُوا بِالْمِلْحِ فِي أَوَّلِ اَلطَّعَامِ فَلَوْ عَلِمَ اَلنَّاسُ مَا فِي اَلْمِلْحِ لاَخْتَارُوهُ عَلَى اَلتِّرْيَاقِ اَلْمُجَرَّبِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4259 - Commander of the Faithful (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Begin with salt at the start of the meal, for if people knew what is in salt, they would prefer it over the tried remedy."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4260 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Wahb ibn Abd Rabbih who said: I saw Abu Abdillah (as) using a toothpick, so I looked at him.
Imam (as) said: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) used a toothpick, and it purifies the mouth."
records.arabicText: 4260 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ وَهْبِ بْنِ عَبْدِ رَبِّهِ قَالَ : رَأَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَتَخَلَّلُ فَنَظَرْتُ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ "إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ كَانَ يَتَخَلَّلُ وَ هُوَ يُطَيِّبُ اَلْفَمَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4260 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Wahb ibn Abd Rabbih who said: I saw Abu Abdillah (as) using a toothpick, so I looked at him.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) used a toothpick, and it purifies the mouth."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4261 - And in another narration: "Indeed, it is from the rights of the guest that a toothpick is prepared for him."
records.arabicText: 4261 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : "إِنَّ مِنْ حَقِّ اَلضَّيْفِ أَنْ يُعَدَّ لَهُ اَلْخِلاَلُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4261 - And in another narration:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, it is from the rights of the guest that a toothpick is prepared for him."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4262 - Imam (as) said: "Whatever you roll around with your tongue and remove, swallow it, and whatever you remove with a toothpick, discard it."
records.arabicText: 4262 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَا أَدَرْتَ عَلَيْهِ لِسَانَكَ فَأَخْرَجْتَهُ فَابْلَعْهُ وَ مَا أَخْرَجْتَهُ بِالْخِلاَلِ فَارْمِ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4262 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whatever you roll around with your tongue and remove, swallow it, and whatever you remove with a toothpick, discard it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4263 - Safwan al-Jammal narrated from Abi Ghurrah al-Khurasani who said that Abu Abdillah (as) said: "Performing ablution before and after eating removes poverty."
records.arabicText: 4263 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ اَلْجَمَّالُ عَنْ أَبِي غُرَّةَ اَلْخُرَاسَانِيِّ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْوُضُوءُ قَبْلَ اَلطَّعَامِ وَ بَعْدَهُ يَذْهَبَانِ بِالْفَقْرِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4263 - Safwan al-Jammal narrated from Abi Ghurrah al-Khurasani who said that Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Performing ablution before and after eating removes poverty."
